seo.pageTitle: Jeju Cruise Shore Excursion Small-Group Tour | East or South Route by Port | AtoC Korea
seo.metaDescription: A Jeju cruise shore excursion small-group tour designed for cruise passengers. Route changes by docking port: Jeju Port guests visit Seongsan Ilchulbong, Seopjikoji, Seongeup Folk Village, and Dongmun Market, while Gangjeong Port guests visit 1100 Highland, Jusangjeolli Cliff, Cheonjiyeon Falls, and Seogwipo Olle Market.

catalog_card.title: Jeju Cruise Shore Excursion Small-Group Tour
catalog_card.subtitle: Port-based East or South Jeju sightseeing with a smaller-group feel built around your ship’s actual docking and departure window.
catalog_card.shortCardDescription: A Jeju shore excursion small-group tour for cruise guests arriving at either Jeju Port or Seogwipo Gangjeong Cruise Port. The east route focuses on Jeju’s UNESCO and heritage icons, while the south route favors mountain views, cliffs, waterfalls, and a traditional market finish.

hero.tagline: Built for cruise timing rather than generic day-tour timing, this Jeju shore excursion runs in a smaller-group format, adjusts its route by docking port, and aims to return you comfortably before all-aboard time.

galleryItems.atmosphere: Jeju’s strongest UNESCO-facing visual payoff for cruise guests docking on the north side
galleryItems.alt: Seongsan Ilchulbong for an east Jeju cruise shore excursion

galleryItems.atmosphere: A south-side port base that naturally connects to Jungmun and central Seogwipo
galleryItems.alt: Gangjeong cruise port for a Jeju shore excursion

galleryItems.atmosphere: Open coastal walking with long sea views and a clear eastern Jeju feel
galleryItems.alt: Coastal scenery similar to Seopjikoji in Jeju

galleryItems.atmosphere: A classic volcanic-coast stop with strong visual reward in a short visit window
galleryItems.alt: Basalt cliff scenery similar to Jusangjeolli on Jeju Island

itineraryStops.name: Jeju Port Route
itineraryStops.category: East Jeju UNESCO and Heritage Route
itineraryStops.description: When your ship docks at Jeju Port on the north side, the route heads east. It focuses on Seongsan Ilchulbong, Seopjikoji, Seongeup Folk Village, and Dongmun Market, which is a logical finish near the old city and port return corridor.
highlights: Best fit for Jeju Port dockings
Strong UNESCO and first-time Jeju landmark appeal
Mix of nature, heritage, and market browsing
Meal or snack time is usually built around Seongsan or Dongmun depending on timing
timeUsed: Drive east for the main scenic block
Finish near the old-city market area before port return
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: From Jeju Port, an eastern route gives stronger sightseeing density than crossing all the way south and back during a cruise call.

visitBasics.hours: Adjusted to the ship’s actual call time
visitBasics.closed: Some attraction schedules vary by season and operating conditions
visitBasics.walking: Moderate overall, with the strongest climb at Seongsan Ilchulbong

convenience.restroom: Available at major attractions and market areas
convenience.parking: Cruise coach access planned around each stop

smartNotes.photo: This route gives the strongest east-side photo mix: crater, cape, thatched village details, and market atmosphere.
smartNotes.facilities: Facilities are straightforward at the main attractions, with the most food flexibility near Seongsan and Dongmun.
smartNotes.tip: The climb at Seongsan is the one stop that changes the route’s physical feel the most.

itineraryStops.name: Gangjeong Port Route
itineraryStops.description: When your ship docks at Seogwipo Gangjeong Cruise Port on the south side, the route stays south and southwest. It connects 1100 Highland, Jusangjeolli Cliff, Cheonjiyeon Falls, and Seogwipo Olle Market for a compact but visually strong shore excursion.
highlights: Best fit for Gangjeong dockings
Mountain, cliff, waterfall, and market variety in one route
Good visual payoff without an extremely long transfer pattern
Food and snack time usually works best around Olle Market
timeUsed: Run a scenery-focused south route with manageable transfers
Return to the port with buffer before all-aboard
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: From Gangjeong Port, staying in the south avoids wasting a cruise call on long cross-island mileage and gives a more reliable return buffer.

visitBasics.hours: Adjusted to the ship’s actual call time
visitBasics.closed: Some attraction schedules vary by season and weather

convenience.restroom: Available at major attractions and market areas
convenience.parking: Cruise coach access planned around each stop

smartNotes.photo: This route leans into broad viewpoints, cliff geometry, water, and a lively local-market ending.
smartNotes.facilities: Olle Market is the strongest stop for flexible food and souvenir time.
smartNotes.tip: 1100 Highland is the most weather-sensitive scenic stop on this route.

routeShapeIntro.title: How the excursion works
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: This is not a generic fixed-time day tour. The departure is usually set for around one hour after docking, and return is usually targeted for around one hour before your ship’s departure. If the ship departs late at night, operational planning aims for return by around 19:00.

bestFor: Cruise passengers who want a more personal group format
Travelers who want admission included and terminal-based meeting
lessIdeal: Guests wanting a fully private custom pace
Travelers avoiding stairs or uphill walking entirely
Visitors hoping to combine east and south Jeju in one short cruise call
Anyone wanting a fully indoor rainy-day itinerary

routeLogicSections.title: Why cruise guests need a different route logic

items.label: The route is chosen by port, not just by popularity
items.detail: Jeju has two cruise ports on different sides of the island, so the smartest shore excursion is the one that matches the docking side rather than forcing a long cross-island detour.

items.label: Return buffer matters as much as sightseeing value
items.detail: The product is designed around the ship’s call window, with return generally planned about one hour before departure rather than squeezing in one more risky stop.

items.label: Smaller groups make cruise-day coordination smoother
items.detail: On a short port call, a smaller group usually means clearer guide communication, easier regrouping, and less friction at major sightseeing stops.

routeLogicSections.title: How the walking load actually feels

items.label: The Jeju Port route is more physically uneven
items.detail: Seongsan Ilchulbong is the most demanding stop in either route because of its uphill climb and stairs, while the other east-side stops feel lighter.

items.label: The Gangjeong route is steadier overall
items.detail: 1100 Highland, Jusangjeolli, Cheonjiyeon, and Olle Market usually feel more evenly manageable than a route anchored by Seongsan.

items.label: Both routes are still sightseeing-first, not trekking-first
items.detail: Even when there is some walking, the product is designed as a shore excursion with manageable stop lengths rather than a hiking tour.

routeLogicSections.title: When timing shifts on cruise days

items.label: Disembarkation speed can move departure earlier or later
items.detail: The scheduled bus departure is usually set around one hour after docking, but actual movement can change if guests clear the ship earlier or terminal flow runs slower.

items.label: Weather affects scenic stops differently by route
items.detail: The east route is most exposed at Seopjikoji and Seongsan, while the south route is most exposed at 1100 Highland and Jusangjeolli.

items.label: Meal time is flexible rather than fixed
items.detail: Lunch is not included, and free time to eat is usually arranged at Seongsan, Dongmun Market, or Olle Market depending on the route and the day’s timing.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Terminal Meeting and Return
practicalAccordionItems.preview: 2 cruise terminals, inside-terminal meeting, same-port return
content: Jeju Port meeting point: inside Jeju Port International Passenger Terminal, near the arrival hall exit
Gangjeong meeting point: inside Seogwipo Gangjeong Cruise Terminal, near the arrival hall exit
Please look for the tour sign shown in your pre-arrival message
Final meeting details are usually reconfirmed about 4 days before arrival
Departure is usually planned for around 1 hour after the ship docks
Return is usually planned for around 1 hour before the ship departs
For late-night departures, operational planning usually aims for return by around 19:00
Return drop-off is at the same terminal where your ship is docked

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking and Terrain
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Moderate overall, but route difficulty changes by port
content: Jeju Port route: Seongsan Ilchulbong is the most demanding stop because of the uphill path and stairs
Jeju Port route: Seopjikoji, Seongeup Folk Village, and Dongmun Market are easier than Seongsan
Gangjeong route: 1100 Highland is light walking, Jusangjeolli and Cheonjiyeon are easy to moderate, and Olle Market is easy
Comfortable walking shoes are strongly recommended for both routes

practicalAccordionItems.title: Weather and Cruise-Day Adjustments
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Port timing and weather can affect pacing, stop order, or stay length
content: Light rain: the excursion usually still operates
Strong wind or reduced visibility can shorten exposed scenic stops
Cruise disembarkation flow and road traffic may affect exact departure and return timing
If a stop becomes unsafe or operationally unsuitable, the guide may adjust stay length or stop order to protect return-to-ship timing

practicalAccordionItems.title: Payments and On-the-Day Spending
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Cards are widely accepted, but some market vendors may still prefer cash
content: Most shops accept credit cards
Some street-food stalls and traditional market vendors may prefer cash
A currency exchange machine may be available near the terminal exit, but lines can be long
A physical passport may be required if you need to exchange currency

practicalAccordionItems.title: What’s Included / Not Included
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Guide, driver, transport, and admission included
content: Professional English-speaking guide
Shared small-group transportation
Cruise-terminal pickup and return
Admission fees to scheduled attractions
Lunch is not included
Tips are not included
Personal expenses, snacks, and optional shopping are not included

bookingTrustItems.title: Small group at the port
bookingTrustItems.description: Meet your van at Jeju or Gangjeong with ship-aware timing

bookingTrustItems.title: Port-specific route
bookingTrustItems.description: Itinerary switches with east vs. south docking

bookingTrustItems.title: More room than a bus
bookingTrustItems.description: Fewer guests, easier coordination on a tight port day

bookingSupportSteps.title: Leave contact details you actually use
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Add a phone number, WhatsApp number, and LINE ID or add-friend link you check every day—especially when gangway times are announced late.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Confirmation email first
bookingSupportSteps.detail: You get a booking confirmation by email: your port-based route, price, and small-group van logistics for the shore day.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Your guide contacts you
bookingSupportSteps.detail: The day before (or as soon as your ship’s timing is clear), your guide reaches you on WhatsApp, LINE, or phone about the exact port meet-up, van location, and how it fits the ship’s schedule.

bookingSupportSteps.title: At the cruise terminal
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Meet your guide at the agreed point with time for port procedures. The small-group van runs on a tight shore window; your guide keeps return timing with a safety margin for the ship.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Back to the ship & support
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Drop-off is planned for the cruise terminal or agreed point. Message us if you need help after the shore day.

seasonalVariations.name: Spring
seasonalVariations.description: Clearer skies and softer temperatures usually help both routes, especially Seopjikoji, Seongsan, and the south-coast scenic stops.

seasonalVariations.name: Summer
seasonalVariations.description: The routes stay scenic, but heat and humidity can make exposed coastal stops feel more demanding than they look on paper.

seasonalVariations.name: Autumn
seasonalVariations.description: Autumn usually gives some of the cleanest sightseeing conditions for shore excursions, especially when visibility is good at 1100 Highland and Seongsan.

seasonalVariations.name: Winter
seasonalVariations.description: Winter light can be beautiful, but wind and visibility affect the route more strongly, especially at 1100 Highland, Seopjikoji, and Seongsan.

faqItems.a: The route depends on your ship’s docking port. Ships docking at Jeju Port use the east route, while ships docking at Seogwipo Gangjeong Cruise Port use the south route.

faqItems.q: What time does the tour start and end?
faqItems.a: Departure is usually planned for around one hour after docking, and return is usually planned for around one hour before departure. For late-night sailings, planning usually targets a return by around 19:00.
No. Lunch is not included. Free time to eat is usually arranged at Seongsan, Dongmun Market, or Olle Market depending on the route and timing.
Yes. Admission to attractions is included in the tour fee for this shore excursion product.

faqItems.q: What if my ship is delayed or terminal flow is slow?
faqItems.a: Cruise-day operations are handled around the actual ship schedule, terminal flow, and road conditions. The guide may adjust timing or stay length to protect the return-to-ship buffer.

routeVariants.title: Jeju Port East Route

dockingPort.name: Jeju Port International Passenger Terminal
dockingPort.meetingPoint: Inside the terminal, near the arrival hall exit

routeVariants.summary: The north-port route is built for cruise guests docking in Jeju City. It moves east for the strongest UNESCO-facing stop mix, then finishes with market time closer to the port-side urban area.

stops.name: Seongsan Ilchulbong (Sunrise Peak)
stops.description: Seongsan Ilchulbong is the flagship stop on the Jeju Port route. It is the most iconic east-side landmark and the strongest single stop for first-time visitors who want a clear Jeju highlight rather than a minor viewpoint.
highlights: UNESCO World Natural Heritage landmark
The route’s strongest iconic sightseeing stop
High visual payoff for cruise guests with limited time
Most physically demanding stop on this route

visitBasics.hours: Seasonal hours vary; tickets close before summit closing time
visitBasics.closed: Closed on the first Monday of each month or the next day if Monday is a public holiday
visitBasics.walking: Moderate to challenging uphill with stairs

stops.name: Seopjikoji
stops.description: Seopjikoji adds a clean, open coastal contrast after Seongsan. It works especially well on cruise tours because the scenery reads quickly and clearly without needing a long museum-style visit.
highlights: Open sea views with Seongsan-facing coastal atmosphere
Short but photogenic stop
Good visual contrast after the volcanic cone stop
Weather exposure is the main comfort variable

visitBasics.hours: Open area; operations can vary by day and weather

stops.name: Seongeup Folk Village
stops.description: Seongeup brings traditional Jeju stone-wall lanes, thatched-roof houses, and living heritage into the route. It gives the east course a cultural anchor rather than making it purely scenic.
highlights: Important Folklore Cultural Heritage site
A living village rather than a purely staged museum setting
Adds cultural balance to a landmark-heavy route
Good for storytelling about old Jeju life

visitBasics.hours: Open village environment; local business hours vary

stops.name: Dongmun Traditional Market
stops.category: Traditional Market and Flexible Meal Stop
stops.description: Dongmun Market is the logical finish for Jeju Port calls because it combines snack or light meal flexibility, souvenir browsing, and easy access back toward the port-side urban area.
highlights: Jeju’s largest and oldest permanent market
Flexible stop for food, snacks, and souvenirs
Good last-stop energy before returning to the ship
A practical urban finish for north-port calls

visitBasics.hours: Open year-round; individual vendor hours vary

routeVariants.title: Gangjeong Port South Route

dockingPort.name: Seogwipo Gangjeong Cruise Terminal
dockingPort.meetingPoint: Inside the terminal, near the arrival hall exit
dockingPort.returnPolicy: Usually around 1 hour before departure, or around 19:00 for late-night sailings

routeVariants.summary: The south-port route stays efficient by focusing on 1100 Highland, Jungmun scenery, central Seogwipo waterfall atmosphere, and a market finish close to the south-side urban core.

stops.name: 1100 Highland
stops.description: 1100 Highland works well for Gangjeong arrivals because it gives a Hallasan-side visual change without turning the day into a mountain hike. It is light in walking but highly weather-dependent.
highlights: One of Jeju’s best drive-up Hallasan viewpoints
Short stop with strong visual reward on clear days
Good contrast to later coast and waterfall stops
Most weather-sensitive stop on this route

stops.name: Jusangjeolli Cliff
stops.description: Jusangjeolli is one of the best short scenic stops for a cruise-format bus tour. The volcanic rock columns and sea backdrop make the visual payoff immediate and easy to understand even in a short visit window.
highlights: One of Jeju’s signature volcanic coastal sights
High photo payoff in a short stop
Strong first-time landmark value
Exposure to wind is the key comfort variable

stops.name: Cheonjiyeon Falls
stops.description: Cheonjiyeon adds a greener and softer mid-route rhythm after mountain and coastal scenery. The paved approach and famous waterfall basin make it one of the more broadly accessible scenic stops.
highlights: Classic Seogwipo waterfall stop
Easy scenic path with strong atmosphere
Suitable for many mixed-age groups
A calmer contrast to cliff and viewpoint stops

visitBasics.hours: Open until 22:00; daytime operations apply to most tour visits

stops.name: Seogwipo Maeil Olle Market
stops.category: Traditional Market and Flexible Food Stop
stops.description: Olle Market works especially well as the closing stop on the Gangjeong route because it combines food flexibility, souvenir browsing, and a lively local finish before the return drive to the terminal.
highlights: Seogwipo’s most iconic permanent traditional market
Good for snacks, light meals, and local treats
A practical last stop for south-port calls
Less physically demanding than the earlier scenic stops

visitBasics.hours: Open year-round; night-market activity starts later in the day

walking_notes: The product is sightseeing-first, but the physical feel changes by docking port.
The Jeju Port route is more demanding because Seongsan Ilchulbong includes uphill walking and stairs.
The Gangjeong route is steadier overall, while the smaller-group format usually makes regrouping easier and the pace feel smoother.
Comfortable walking shoes are strongly recommended for cruise-day pacing.

matching_metadata.note: Scores were tuned for a cruise shore-excursion small-group format where return-to-ship safety and port-based routing still matter most, but comfort, guide access, and regrouping efficiency score higher than in a standard bus format. Walking and weather fit were kept conservative because the Jeju Port route includes Seongsan Ilchulbong and both routes include exposed outdoor stops.

departure.name: Jeju Port International Passenger Terminal
departure.note: Meet inside the terminal near the arrival hall exit. Departure is usually about 1 hour after docking.

departure.name: Seogwipo Gangjeong Cruise Terminal
departure.note: Meet inside the terminal near the arrival hall exit. Departure is usually about 1 hour after docking.

return.name: Jeju Port International Passenger Terminal
return.note: Used when the ship is docked at Jeju Port.

return.name: Seogwipo Gangjeong Cruise Terminal
return.note: Used when the ship is docked at Seogwipo Gangjeong.

notes: The exact terminal used depends on your ship’s docking port.
Final sign and meeting guidance is usually reconfirmed about 4 days before arrival.
Return is planned with a ship-safety buffer rather than at the last possible minute.
If the sailing departs late at night, planning usually aims for return by around 19:00.
